GN[Tournament Game: CGA Open Feb 9x9 (4177) R:1 (allfather vs Yukontodd)]
C[Yukontodd: Hi, allfather!  Have a good game.  And, btw, want to admin on the Canadian group?
C[allfather: That would be fantastic...  I could then disseminate more CGA news should something come up.
allfather: There is a Winter Cup in Montreal on the 21st.
C[Yukontodd: You do some work for the cga?  That's even better.  Were you at the open this year?  I played there as an 8k.
Yukontodd: As one of the few 40+ year olds under dan level, it was great fun watching the kids outplay me at the open.:)
C[allfather: I didn't make it to the Open this year. I have been in the past.  It is quite the event but travel is reserved for my children these days.
allfather: I was tournament director for the open for one year, and may do it again in the future.  It is a great event but no one seems to like travelling to Winnipeg.
C[Yukontodd: So, CGA ranked tourneys on OGS happening anytime soon?:)

allfather: I put them all on a scheduled start time for tall the free tourneys.  I am not sure if any official sanctioned tournaments will be available soon.  I am still working with the tournament system to lean the viability of large scale tournaments (with divisions) and their prizes.  After that it will still need authorization from the oversight comittee.
allfather: If I do apply for the rights to host the CanOpen, I may look at IRL signups to play online (HERE) in a timed style to see what kind of global interest we can accrue.
allfather: This would require larger cash prizes a few sub directors to make sure all happens in order within the selected time frame.

C[Yukontodd: The CGA was supposed to be opening up the tournament and/or ratings system at some point this year (or last year*sigh*), so in theory we could just run live tournaments on OGS that fit their criteria and have tournament results and members CGA ratings impacted appropriately.
C[allfather: The Conversation on facebook is really heating up.  I feel that the online work will be the place to go.  With careful review of every game I am sure we can get some good results.

allfather: The tournaments I have up now are for testing purposes.  Getting a feel for the system and how it may be used to benefit us.  On top of that, I need a way to get the sign up money so real monetary prizes may be assigned.
C[Yukontodd: I should follow facebook more, apparently.:)
Yukontodd: Thanks for the game, and the chat.:)

C[Yukontodd: hum.  After p18, l19 j19 k17 then j16 cuts black's liberties in half, I think?

C[Yukontodd: problem is I'll lose the half of that group on the right, but maybe I'll salvage enough.  Unless I kill the group below, which I don't think I will, next has to be cutting off the bone crushing group, if I'm to have a chance.
C[Yukontodd: Bah, that line just isn't going to work.  Time to cut the bone breaker out of the herd, see what comes of pressing it.
C[Yukontodd: I think I can either connect out or make a bulky five.  After A13, c11 and c12 and b10 are miai, one gets me bulky five the other a tiger's mouth I can cut with?
Yukontodd: Might be short of liberties, though.  Guess I'll find out.
C[Yukontodd: yeah, he'll just play b11.  b10 doesn't work without a tiger's mouth.  At least it looks like I got the middle out of the deal, but I need more.
Yukontodd: I'll try some reductions, if I can, but I don't know if I can pull off playing deep enough to tip the balance.
Yukontodd: Let's go back to the upper group first, on second thought.  It's not going to get any better, now.
Yukontodd: First, a patch in the middle, so maybe I can play from the outside on the left after he has to rescue his top right.
C[Yukontodd: M16 looks tempting for either one of us, too.

Yukontodd: Oh, dammit, I saw that too!  I should have played a13!  I assumed he'd play a13, and when he didn't, I was blind to the move myself!  Damn!   Well, he has to answer me anyway, as long as I have sente, I have to get back to A13 before he gets there!
C[Yukontodd: now let's see if we can't crush the bone crusher after all.
Yukontodd: If I can manage to play out the right side and maybe slay a dragon, and get back and make an eye with m16, I wonder if I can kill two.

C[Yukontodd: nope doesn't work.  I should have played the ko at a11 (which I didn't see until after I played because I'm lazy and didn't READ), then threatened to live with the other group above...one of the groups probably would have made it!
Yukontodd: Nah, it probably couldn`t have saved the side anyway.
C[Yukontodd: I had to kill one of them to have a chance, I think.
Yukontodd: Best I have now seems to leave me 2 liberties too short.:)
Yukontodd: When things settle down over the holidays, I'd love to find time to play a live game, by the way.:)
Yukontodd: I malkoviched my last desperate tries at killing one of those dragons, by the way, if you'd like a laugh.:)
alrobin: Yeah - saving my C10 group pretty well decided the game for me as I've got a pretty solid grip on your L16 group.  Thanks for the game!
Yukontodd: I malkoviched me trying desperately to figure out a way to kill one or the other of them.  It's funny.  The best I can do is make a ko on top, now, with three liberties still to go.
Yukontodd: Ah, and that's if you're kind enough to leave me the ko.
alrobin: The ko doesn't work as you can't connect at N19
alrobin: It was an interesting game!
bengozen: Have a good game too!
Yukontodd: Pretty good.  Yeah, I like it.
Yukontodd: I haven't played a diagonal fuseki for a long time.:)
bengozen: i definitely dont have much experience with it
Yukontodd: Usually lots of fighting, I think.
C[Yukontodd: I keep forgetting to press submit move.
C[bengozen: Yeah that's the only thing that keeps it from truly feeling like a live game
bengozen: but it does prevent misclicks
C[Yukontodd: Probably a good thing if you try this on your phone.
C[bengozen: the condition moves are pretty cool too
C[Yukontodd: I've been having fun on our other game.  Since I had such a big handicap, I decided to try playing really aggressive and see how it goes.:)
bengozen: yes it's been a lot of fun
C[Yukontodd: What you wrote about, letting go of worry about winning...things have been a lot more fun for me since I started trying that, too.
C[Yukontodd: I think you have to lose a couple ranks, at the point I'm at, before you can gain a couple ranks.  Maybe.  We'll see.
bengozen: the ranks start to fluctuate
bengozen: but you approach the game a whole new way
bengozen: and it becomes far more interesting
Yukontodd: Yep.  This corner is interesting.  I think it's a failure, probably, but it's interesting, so now I'll see what I can do with it.
bengozen: In fact, in my eyes, one of the best compliments I get when people analyze my game is to tell me it's an interesting one
C[Yukontodd: I'm playing one on dragon that would have made me hopping mad before.  A totally unreasonable invasion, but it's interesting and in its fiftieth move or so.
bengozen: Your opponent made a totally unreasonable invasion?
Yukontodd: Yes, but it's close enough that I can't just kill it outright.  It's all over my territory with no eyes!
Yukontodd: wow I butchered this corner.  I should try something different.
bengozen: i dunno if butchered is correct
bengozen: we played a non=joseki sequence
Yukontodd: My last move there was probably silly, but I'm getting paranoid and defensive.:)
bengozen: and you still have the 3-3 invasion
Yukontodd: It's a bamboo jungle, though, isn't it.:)
C[Yukontodd: so, proverb says, if you have four corners, you should resign.
Yukontodd: Another proverb says, if you have no corners, you should resign.  I think they're both aimed at black, though.:)
bengozen: the funny thing is i learned the reverse of that at first
bengozen: hmm... that's an interesting distinction that never occurred to me
C[Yukontodd: I think I should head to bed, though, if it seems an okay place to adjourn to you.:)
bengozen: that's the beauty of this
bengozen: we'll continue this later on
bengozen: thanks for staying up to play!
Yukontodd: Okay, then.  See you next time.:)
C[Yukontodd: Heya!  Your handle, bengozen ... I'm not sure if it indicates an interest in Zen...
Yukontodd: If it does, I'm starting blogging about re-starting my zen practice in a different blog.  Let me know if you're interested in reading it.:)
C[bengozen: absolutely! send me the link whenever you have a chance!
C[Yukontodd: Great!  My zen blog is at http://whitehorsezen.blogspot.ca/
C[bengozen: Nice blog! Looking forward reading more!
C[Yukontodd: Hey, thanks.  Knowing it's getting read is helpful in getting my butt back to the cushions.
C[Yukontodd: Hi!  I totally forgot about this server until I saw a post about it...
Yukontodd: Pretty good.  Finally having a nice warm week-end in the Yukon.:)
C[Yukontodd: So, have you played much realtime on Nova yet?
Yukontodd: No, not yet.  Sign up for the tournament that starts tomorrow?
bengozen: haha i totally would, but still trying to make sure i have a right balance for go
bengozen: definitely still teetering that line
C[Yukontodd: Yeah, I like correspondence games.
bengozen: yeah they're a lot of fun
Yukontodd: Though I hope they make an app.  I really like being able to play dragon games on my phone.
bengozen: that's the only reason i still have a couple of games on there
C[bengozen: the web app for nova isn't all there quite yet
C[Yukontodd: Okay...I should go work on supper for the boys.  See you next time.  I really liked the boxing analogies, by the way.:)
C[Yukontodd: You know, I'd lost track of that until I made my last move.
bengozen: we have a very messy board
Yukontodd: I thought black was all alive after threatening the corner, but it's all totally dead!:)
bengozen: haha. you definitely made me worry
Yukontodd: You must have thought I saw something you didn't.:)
bengozen: Thank you for the game!
bengozen: i should really find out what my rank is
bengozen: but i just don't seem to care enough to play a bunch of ranked games right now
Yukontodd: Yeah.  I'm probably an honest 8 kyu by now, you're probably at least three stones stronger, but that's really both guessing because I need to get myself ranked better too.:)
bengozen: well let me know if you want to play another game or need a break
Yukontodd: Ok...I need breakfast and a strong coffee.  Oh, set up another if you like, I'm always up for correspondence games.:)
bengozen: did you want to do a handicap game?
bengozen: i'm okay with either, whichever you think you'll benefit from more
bengozen: let's start with two stones then
bengozen: and then every two games
bengozen: if someone wins twice in a row
bengozen: then we up/lower the handicap
bengozen: i'll send you the invite
bengozen: hope you have a good day!
Yukontodd: It's fun to give a stronger player handicap sometimes.
bengozen: well if you tire of it
bengozen: we can always switch back
Yukontodd: You should try it.  Just make sure you resign before it's totally boring for them.  You get to see how they handle the extra stones.

C[Yukontodd: Hi!  I forgot about this server for a while.  Still here?:)
C[rhubarb: yup, i'm here. luckily the time limit on this one is super long :)
Yukontodd: You know, I used to live in the sister town (about 3 kms away) from a little place that called itself ""Rhubarb Capital of Ontario"":)
C[Yukontodd: The town was Shedden, though it didn't have much to do with rhubarb until long after I lived in the area.  I grew up down the road a bit in Fingal.  Tiny town of about 400 maybe.:)
C[rhubarb: i keep thinking i should eat more rhubarb on account of my handle, but i never really have

rhubarb: had a friend who worked as an extra, who said they had half the extras say ""rhubarb rhubarb rhubarb"" and the other have ""harglebargle harglebargle"" to produce the sound of background conversation
rhubarb: and that's more or less why i chose this when i had to choose something :P

C[Yukontodd: Rhubarb grows wherever you plant it.  It's hard to not grow rhubarb sometimes.  Heck, I grow rhubarb here.  So it's kinda funny to say your town is known for its rhubarb.  Though different strains do taste better or worse, I suppose.
Yukontodd: I suppose they could be the harglebargle capital of Ontario.  That'd be slightly more interesting, though.  Shedden was known for the ""Shedden massacre"" more than its rhubarb.
Yukontodd: I used to carry out groceries for one of the gang leaders involved in that.  Nice guy.  Except of course, apparently he wasn't.
Yukontodd: I must be tired, I'm free-associating.  Good night.:)
C[rhubarb: i guess rhubarb is probably better for tourism than massacres... then again, maybe even that's not true.
C[rhubarb: thanks for the game! v well played.

Yukontodd: Those last few moves after m18 on move 123 just fell into place like a runaway train for me.  Best play of the year on my side, that bit...but I DID have a four stone handicap, and that's always hard to begin with.

C[Yukontodd: Oh, sorry.  I didn't say hello.  Hi!:)
C[hdci: That happens to me also, sometimes ! just forgetting to press the Enter key :)
C[hdci: Wher are you from in Canada ?
C[Yukontodd: Hi.:)  I'm from Whitehorse, Yukon Territory.
C[hdci: That's far in the north, isn't it ? Above the polar circle ?
Yukontodd: Well, part of the territory is, but Whitehorse is about six hours drive south of the circle.  Still doesn't get dark at night, right now, though.:)
C[Yukontodd: The sunset turns immediately into a sunrise.  Pretty enough to break your heart when you get a good one.
hdci: that must be impressive ! I lived in Ottawa long ago (92). I remember that winter was quite cold - how is it in Whitehorse ?
C[Yukontodd: Much colder.  Not as much snow, as a rule, though, and dry cold.  Usually hits -40 in a normal year.

C[hdci: When I lived in Ottawa, it the cold reached down to -50. But it was because of the wind. However, I felt it much less than in Paris (when it's -1 or -2 in Paris, it is very cold - too much water in the air...)
Yukontodd: At -40, there's perfect calm, too, no wind.  Wind warms it up, technically.  Better to be -35 or -40 than have -30 plus windchill, I think.
C[hdci: Yes, The wind enters in whatever clothes you wear...
Yukontodd: At -40, your tires are squared off in the morning.  Thump thump thump until they warm up.
hdci: ""square off"" : I don't understand this word ?
Yukontodd: Flat on the bottom.  They freeze that way.
hdci: I'm living near Paris in France, but I used to live in the mountain (the Alps) 2 years ago. Too bad I had to move
C[Yukontodd: My wife has an aunt living in the south, in the Pyranees (if I spelled that right).
C[Yukontodd: Nice chatting with you!  Makes the game more fun.:)
hdci: Pyrénées with the accents on the e-s. That's between France and Spain. Nice area too :)
hdci: Yeah. I think it's over, nice game :)
Yukontodd: Congrats on a nice close one.:)
hdci: Very tied indeed. I think you might have win, a4 wasn't the right move from my guess
Yukontodd: See you around, I hope. Gotta run.
